title: Industry Research Framework Research Quality Rubric
scoring_note: Use this for human review after the offline runner. The automated score catches conformance regressions; this rubric judges whether the work is actually worth reading.

dimensions.name: brief_and_scope
dimensions.excellent: Objective, reader, scope, exclusions, evidence standard, and depth are explicit before collection.
dimensions.poor: The agent starts writing without clarifying or recording what the report is for.

dimensions.name: evidence_discipline
dimensions.excellent: Facts, source claims, interpretation, author judgment, and uncertainty remain distinct.
dimensions.poor: PR, media summaries, community comments, and author opinion collapse into one unsupported argument.

dimensions.name: mechanism_and_synthesis
dimensions.excellent: The report explains mechanisms, causality, tradeoffs, and implications across sources.
dimensions.poor: The report lists companies or sources without producing a thesis.

dimensions.name: counter_evidence
dimensions.excellent: Strong alternative explanations, adoption friction, and failure modes are integrated into the argument.
dimensions.poor: The report only supports its initial thesis and hides uncertainty.

dimensions.name: depth
dimensions.excellent: Central units receive enough detail, and thin sections are expanded before reader review.
dimensions.poor: The output is compressed, generic, or shorter than the stated depth budget.

dimensions.name: reader_quality
dimensions.excellent: The final prose reads like an authored research article or report.
dimensions.poor: The final prose reads like a process transcript with source IDs, audit labels, or agent workflow language.

pass_guidance: A serious framework change should not be accepted only because the automated runner passes. At least one human-reviewed output should pass this rubric.
